creator: bz@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Mon, 14 Dec 2009, Matthew Seaman wrote:

>
>> Number:         141457
>> Category:       kern
>> Synopsis:       page fault while in kernel mode on VIMAGE enabled kernel
...
> <118>Starting pflog.
> <118>Enabling pf
> <118>Dec 14 08:46:13 pflogd[411]: [priv]: msg PRIV_OPEN_LOG received
>
>
> Fatal trap 12: page fault while in kernel mode
> cpuid = 0; apic id = 00
> fault virtual address	= 0x18
> fault code		= supervisor read, page not present
> instruction pointer	= 0x20:0xc0928873
> stack pointer	        = 0x28:0xe9495a7c
> frame pointer	        = 0x28:0xe9495a90
> code segment		= base 0x0, limit 0xfffff, type 0x1b
> 			= DPL 0, pres 1, def32 1, gran 1
> processor eflags	= interrupt enabled, resume, IOPL = 0
> current process		= 420 (pfctl)
> trap number		= 12
> panic: page fault
> cpuid = 0
> Uptime: 3m22s
> Physical memory: 3051 MB
> Dumping 96 MB: 81 65 49 33 17 1


neither 9-CURRENT nor stable/8 has a virtualized pf(4).  You'd need to
remove that from your kernel and not load it to avoid any trouble.

-- 
Bjoern A. Zeeb         It will not break if you know what you are doing.
